brief_justification: Paper A derives a Weierstrass-product factorization of the twisted Selberg zeta function in terms of Laplace resonances, Barnes G- and gamma functions on hyperbolic orbisurfaces — spectral geometry / analytic number theory. Paper B is an empirical bibliometric study computing the ratio of female authors to corresponding authors across 220M publications to quantify the Matilda effect. There is no shared governing process or role-for-role mapping: one is a complex-analytic spectral factorization, the other a statistical measurement of gendered authorship, so the machinery is entirely different.
